chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What determines whether current flows in a circuit?
questions.answer: Current flows only when the circuit is closed and connected to a power source.

questions.question: Why does a fuse protect electrical appliances?
questions.answer: A fuse melts when excessive current flows, breaking the circuit and preventing damage.

questions.question: What happens to the resistance wire in an electric heater?
questions.answer: It becomes very hot due to the heating effect of current.

questions.question: Why does the filament of an electric bulb glow?
questions.answer: The filament becomes white hot due to the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: What happens when a current-carrying wire is placed near a compass?
questions.answer: The compass needle deflects due to the magnetic field around the wire.

questions.question: Why is soft iron used as the core in electromagnets?
questions.answer: Soft iron easily becomes magnetized and quickly loses magnetism when current stops.

questions.question: How does increasing the number of turns in a coil affect an electromagnet?
questions.answer: It increases the strength of the electromagnet.

questions.question: Why does excessive current cause damage in circuits?
questions.answer: It produces excessive heat which can damage wires and appliances.

questions.question: What happens to the magnetic field when electric current stops?
questions.answer: The magnetic field disappears.

questions.question: Why are electromagnets called temporary magnets?
questions.answer: They act as magnets only when electric current flows.

questions.question: How does an electric bell use electromagnetism?
questions.answer: Electric current creates a magnetic field that pulls the hammer to strike the bell.

questions.question: Why are heating appliances made with high-resistance wires?
questions.answer: High resistance wires produce more heat when current flows.

questions.question: What happens if a circuit has no power source?
questions.answer: Electric current cannot flow.

questions.question: Why are electromagnets preferred over permanent magnets in machines?
questions.answer: Their magnetism can be controlled by switching the current on or off.

questions.question: What role does insulation play in electrical wiring?
questions.answer: It prevents current leakage and protects users from electric shocks.

questions.question: Why does the magnetic field form around a current-carrying wire?
questions.answer: Moving electric charges produce a magnetic field around the conductor.

questions.question: How does increasing electric current affect heating?
questions.answer: Increasing current increases the heat produced.

questions.question: Why is a fuse placed in series in a circuit?
questions.answer: So that all current passes through it and it can break the circuit when needed.

questions.question: What would happen if an electromagnet used a steel core?
questions.answer: It would remain magnetized even after current stops.

questions.question: Why are electric cranes able to release metal objects easily?
questions.answer: Switching off the current removes the magnetism.

questions.question: What does a battery provide in an electric circuit?
questions.answer: Electrical energy for current flow.

questions.question: Why does an electric iron become hot when switched on?
questions.answer: Electric current produces heat due to resistance in the heating element.

questions.question: How does increasing the number of cells affect a circuit?
questions.answer: It increases the electric current in the circuit.

questions.question: Why are circuit diagrams used in electricity studies?
questions.answer: They represent electric circuits clearly using symbols.

questions.question: Why must electrical appliances be connected properly in circuits?
questions.answer: Proper connections ensure safe and correct functioning of the appliances.
